data.title: LULU Stock: Lululemon Athletica Posts Strong Financial Results, Announces CEO Departure
data.description:
Canada's Lululemon Athletica (LULU) posted strong fiscal third-quarter financial results and announced that its current CEO will depart the company in January 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Vancouver-based Lululemon announced earnings per share (EPS) of $2.59, which beat the consensus forecast on Wall Street of $2.21. Revenue for the three months ended on Nov. 2 totaled $2.57 billion, which topped the $2.48 billion expected among analysts.

Along with the better-than-expected results, Lululemon said that Calvin McDonald will step down as both CEO and a board member on Jan. 31, 2026. He will serve as a senior advisor through March 31 of next year. The company plans to search for a new CEO and board chair Marti Morfitt will assume an expanded role of executive chair, effective immediately.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Hit by Tech Weakness as Jobless Claims Normalize
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) closed Thursday's trading session higher, while the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) ended with a loss. Both ETFs were affected by weakness in the tech and AI sectors after Oracle (ORCL) reported disappointing earnings results, with major players like Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) sustaining losses in sympathy.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Before the market open, the Department of Labor announced that initial jobless claims for the week ended December 6 surged by 44,000 to 236,000, registering the largest weekly rise since 2020 and topping the consensus estimate of 220,000. At the same time, initial claims data for the previous week, which came in at a three-year low, were likely skewed because they included the Thanksgiving holiday.

"Don't read too much into the jump in jobless claims," said Navy Federal Credit Union Chief Economist Heather Long. "Smoothing it out, this still looks like an economy averaging 215,000 to 220,000 new jobless claims a week. That's not a cause for concern." Continuing jobless claims, which track ongoing unemployment benefit recipients, were 1.838 million for the week ended November 22, below the estimate of 1.95 million.

Meanwhile, the U.S. trade deficit improved to $52.8 billion in September, marking its lowest level in over five years and below the estimate of $63.1 billion. However, the year-to-date deficit is still 17.2%, or $112.6 billion, higher than the same period in 2024. During the month, exports of non-monetary gold and consumer goods rose, while imports of computers, computer accessories, and electronics fell. Notably, the U.S. trade deficit with China fell by $4 billion to $11.4 billion, while its deficit with Switzerland during the previous month changed to a surplus of $6.6 billion. The deficit is calculated by subtracting exports from imports.

The falling deficit comes despite November's tariff revenue falling to $30.76 billion from $31.35 billion in October. President Trump has toned down his aggressive tariff stance in recent weeks and signed an executive order last month that excludes certain food and beverage items from reciprocal tariffs, including coffee, tomatoes, oranges, and beef. Trump also told Politico that he is considering additional tariff exclusions on consumer staple products.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.21% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.35%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Wall Street Cuts Oracle (ORCL) Price Targets on Q2 Revenue Miss, Weak Guidance, Rising AI Spending
data.description:
Wall Street analysts appear to be highly disappointed by software giant Oracle's (ORCL) mixed Q2FY26 results and weak revenue guidance. While the artificial intelligence (AI) player beat earnings estimates, it fell short on revenue. Investors are showing skepticism about the company's large AI investments. ORCL stock is down 11.4% in pre-market trading, dragging down AI-related names, including Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and CoreWeave (CRWV).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

One key highlight was the 438% year-over-year rise in Remaining Performance Obligations (RPOs), which jumped to $523 billion. This increase included major new deals from clients like Meta (META) and Nvidia.

Analysts Express Concern Over AI Prospects

Following the results, analysts promptly revised their forecasts and price targets for Oracle stock, while maintaining Buy ratings.

Stifel Nicolaus analyst Brad Reback slashed his price target from $350 to $275, implying 23.3% upside potential. Reback stated that Oracle's earnings and outlook renewed worries about increasing expenses and short-term profit challenges.

He emphasized that the modest 15% quarter-on-quarter growth in RPOs was overshadowed by a much higher-than-expected increase in capital expenditure guidance. Oracle now projects about $50 billion in spending for fiscal 2026, approximately $15 billion more than earlier forecasts.

Reback also highlighted that Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) revenue of roughly $4.1 billion met expectations, but earnings per share, excluding Ampere's contribution, appeared to fall short by $0.10 to $0.15.

Additionally, Reback pointed out that management reassured investors by reaffirming its commitment to maintaining investment-grade credit and noted multiple funding options, including customers supplying their own chips. The high capital expenditure is expected to accelerate Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) growth and boost EPS in fiscal 2027.

Similarly, Bank of America Securities analyst Bradley Sills lowered his price target from $368 to $300, implying 34.5% upside potential. Sills noted that Oracle is currently in the most intense phase of expanding its AI infrastructure. However, he views the current gap between AI spending and revenue as a temporary investment phase rather than a fundamental problem.

Is Oracle a Long-Term Buy?

Analysts remain divided about Oracle's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, ORCL stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 23 Buys, 11 Holds, and one Sell rating. The average Oracle price target of $346.11 implies 55.2% upside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, ORCL stock has surged 35.2%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Gold Rallies to One-Month High Following Fed Rate Cut
data.description:
Gold futures are trading higher on Thursday and have reclaimed the $4,300 level after the Fed voted to cut interest rates by 25 bps. The precious metal is up by 4.7% over the past month and is currently trading at a one-month high.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Lower interest rates typically benefit gold because it doesn't pay out interest. Another way to put it is the opportunity cost of holding gold relative to interest-bearing assets, like Treasuries, goes down with lower rates.

Gold on Track for Best Year Since 1979

The Fed has lowered rates by a total of 75 bps this year in three separate Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings and is scheduled to meet again on January 28.

"Inflation hasn't really come back down to the Fed's 2% target, so, when you're lowering rates in an inflationary environment that is still not optimum, and that's very bullish for gold," said Marex analyst Edward Meir.

Spot gold (XAUUSD) has returned 60% year-to-date and is on track to cement its best year since 1979. The World Gold Council expects upside of 15% to 30% in 2026, driven by geopolitical risks, lower rates, and demand from exchange-traded funds (ETFs).

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Who is the Current Odds-On Favorite to Win the Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) Bid War?
data.description:
The race to buy entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) is quite possibly more entertaining than anything any of the studios involved has put out in months. And right now, Polymarket has odds on the favorite to win. Is it Netflix (NFLX)? Is it Paramount Skydance (PSKY)? Only time will ultimately tell, but Polymarket has a winner projected. Warner shareholders are largely holding their collective breath, as shares dipped fractionally in the closing minutes of Thursday's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The odds break down like so: currently, Paramount is the frontrunner, as 49% of bettors believe that Paramount will come out ahead. Current bets on Paramount to win total $7,507. While fewer bettors believe that Netflix will come out ahead—only 36% of bettors have money down on that angle—they are willing to put significantly more money where their mouths are, collectively. There are $10,948 worth of bets on Netflix to win.

Meanwhile, amazingly, less than 1% of bets are for Comcast (CMCSA) to come back from out of nowhere to step in and buy out Warner. That move is remarkably unlikely, as Comcast has already announced it is out of the running. However, a little under 20% of bettors are putting their money on no one to win and Warner to remain unpurchased.

Canada Gets Concerned

Concerns about the Warner deal are mounting, with theaters pretty unnerved and government agencies raising eyebrows as well. One more sector weighed in, and it might surprise you. The Canadian film industry is concerned about the fate of its own operations if Warner gets sold in either direction.

Canadian producers reported that they were already having a hard time getting anyone at Warner to take their calls after merging with Discovery. Thus, they believe that another merger would make access even harder to arrange. In the end, noted David Cronenberg's long-time producer, Martin Katz, "If there are fewer people to come to, that makes it more difficult to make a sale."

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on eight Buys and 12 Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 136.43% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $22.65 per share implies 23.17% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Evercore, BMO Analysts Slash Oracle Stock (ORCL) Price Target on Q2 Revenue Miss, But Stay Bullish
data.description:
Oracle (ORCL) stock was down 11% on Thursday, at the time of writing, as the database software provider and cloud computing company missed revenue expectations for the second quarter of Fiscal 2026 and increased its full-year capital expenditure outlook. Several analysts, including those at Evercore and BMO, lowered their price targets for ORCL stock amid investor concerns about the company's elevated artificial intelligence (AI) investments.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Analysts Weigh in on Oracle's Q2 Results

Evercore analyst Kirk Materne lowered the price target for Oracle stock to $275 from $385 and reiterated a Buy rating. Among the positives, Materne noted that the $68 billion sequential increase in the company's remaining performance obligations (RPO) and the incremental $4 billion in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) revenue for Fiscal 2027 reflect long-term growth opportunities. Also, management indicated lower-than-expected cash financing needs (less than $100 billion).

Turning to the negatives, the 5-star analyst pointed out that ORCL's Q2 FY26 revenue missed the Street's expectations. Moreover, the incremental $15 billion in capital expenditure for Fiscal 2026 worsens the ongoing debt and margin concerns. While Materne believes that management's updates on financing options and bookings trends were positive, he expects some investors to seek proof of accelerating OCI revenue in the second half of Fiscal 2026 and assurance that elevated spending is translating into reliable growth. In fact, Materne believes that continued OCI revenue acceleration in H2 FY26 will be the next major catalyst, helping reinforce the full-year OCI growth outlook of 77% and further acceleration into Fiscal 2028.

"We don't see the bull/bear tug-of-war getting worked out in the immediate near-term, but as discussed in our preview, we believe the upside potential outstrips the downside risk for those willing to take a 6-12 month view," concluded Materne.

Likewise, BMO Capital analyst Keith Bachman lowered the price target for Oracle stock to $270 from $355 and reaffirmed a Buy rating. The 4-star analyst thinks that ORCL's Q2 FY26 results were "lackluster," mainly due to the Cloud Services revenue, though operating cash flow was positive. Bachman continues to be optimistic about Oracle's positioning in cloud and AI and its full-stack application offering, including database, as more enterprise AI workloads move to production. However, Bachman continues to believe that Oracle's performance is closely tied to OpenAI (PC:OPAIQ), which presents some longer-term risk due to the size of the commitments.

Is ORCL Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Oracle stock based on 23 Buys, 11 Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average ORCL stock price target of $346.11 indicates 74% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Robinhood Markets Stock (HOOD) Sinks 9% as Trading Volumes Decline
data.description:
The stock of Robinhood Markets (HOOD) is down 9% on Dec. 11 after the online brokerage reported a sharp drop in its November trading volumes.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Robinhood, whose trading platform and app are popular with retail investors, reported that its equity trading volumes fell 37% month-over-month during November. Additionally, options volumes declined 28%, and cryptocurrency trading volumes fell 12%.

With both equity and crypto markets weakening during November, assets under custody at Robinhood dropped 5% month-over-month to $325 billion, the first monthly decline since February of this year. The trading platform also said that its funded accounts declined by 130,000.

Robinhood's Net Deposits Rise

Despite the soft trading volumes, November delivered Robinhood's third-best month of the year for net deposits at $7.1 billion, up nearly 27% from October. Earlier this week, Robinhood Markets announced its entry into Indonesia.

Robinhood's predictions market was a standout growth area for the company in November, with three billion event contracts traded, up 20% from October and 500% year-over-year. Robinhood is holding a predictions market event on Dec. 16, which some analysts say could be the next catalyst to lift HOOD stock.

Is HOOD Stock a Buy?

Robinhood's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 14 Buy, five Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average HOOD price target of $151.39 implies 22.57% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 'It's Still Rock-Solid,' Says Bank of America About Oracle Stock
data.description:
Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) stock is feeling the heat on Thursday, tumbling 10% after its fiscal Q2 results failed to win over investors. And if you were among those who rushed to hit the sell button, Bank of America thinks you may have jumped the gun. But before we get to their case, let's break down what caused the sell-off in the first place.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The company just missed on the topline, delivering revenue of $16.06 billion, amounting to a 13.9% year-over-year increase yet falling shy of the consensus estimate by $130 million. On the other hand, adj. EPS came in at $2.26, well above analyst expectations of $1.64. That said, the outperformance was largely driven by the sale of its Ampere chip business to SoftBank, which added $0.91 per diluted share to pretax earnings.

But the disappointment seemed to lie elsewhere. For one, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) revenue increased 69% year-over-year, hitting market expectations of around $4.1 billion but failing to outperform. Moreover, capital expenditures came in at $12 billion, $3.8 billion above consensus, resulting in negative free cash flow of nearly $10 billion for the quarter. During the conference call, management noted that FY26 capital spending (ending May 31) is expected to be $15 billion higher than previously anticipated.

With the company now entering into the "heaviest phase of its AI infrastructure buildout," Bank of America analyst Brad Sills argues that the quarter didn't signal a deterioration in Oracle's business, but rather exposed the "timing mismatch of buildout spend to revenue conversion." Sills pointed to the underwhelming OCI revenue growth and the elevated capex required as Oracle ramps its infrastructure to support the massive $523 billion OCI AI backlog. Still, Sills frames the shortfall as an "investment curve issue rather than a change in fundamentals."

And that's where his thesis lands. Fundamentals "remain strong," he says, and the present weakness is down to elevated capex needed to keep pace with rapid AI demand. Underlying trends are positive: AI demand "continues to swell," large site builds are on schedule, OCI's "fungible architecture" supports diverse compute needs, and the company maintains strong financing access while upholding investment-grade credit.

As for the stock, it has now given back all of its gains "and some" since the September OpenAI announcement, reflecting "skittish investor confidence in Oracle's ability to transform into an AI compute hyperscaler." Yet, Sills thinks there is room for sentiment and estimates to move higher. AI spending remains strong across a wide range of enterprises, and Oracle's applications business is showing "early signs of an AI halo effect," with revenue accelerating to 11%, up 200bps year-over-year.

"We therefore focus on the catalysts that could drive upward stock momentum, namely accelerating revenue conversion, improving visibility into the OCI AI margin profile, and AI-driven momentum across applications and database offerings," the analyst summed up.

Bottom line, Sills assigns ORCL a Buy rating, but given "valuation contraction across the AI cohort," his price objective goes from $368 to $300. Nevertheless, the new target still makes room for 12-month returns of 50%. (To watch Sills' track record, click here)

22 other analysts also take a positive view of Oracle's prospects and with an additional 11 Holds and 1 Sell, the analyst consensus rates the shares a Moderate Buy. There are plenty of gains projected here now; at $346.11, the average price target implies the stock will climb 73% higher over the one-year timeframe. (See ORCL stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Government Shutdown Odds Surge after Senate Rejects ACA Subsidies Extension
data.description:
The odds of the government shutting down again by January 31, 2026, surged to 48% on prediction platform Kalshi after the Senate failed to pass a Democratic bill that would extend Affordable Care Act (ACA) healthcare subsidies for three years. The odds have risen by 8% during the past 24 hours and 17% during the past week.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Extending the subsidies was a major point of contention between the two parties that led to the previous shutdown, which ended on November 12 after lasting for a record 43 days.

ACA Subsidies Extension Bill Falls Short Despite Bipartisan Support

The Senate consists of 53 Republicans and 47 Democrats, and requires 60 votes for a proposal to pass. The ACA extension proposal received 51 "yes" votes, with all Democrats and four Republicans voting in favor. Senate Republicans proposed an alternative bill that would lead to some Americans receiving federal assistance to help pay out-of-pocket healthcare costs, although the measure failed to pass as well with zero Democratic votes.

The ACA open enrollment period for plans that begin on January 1, 2026, is set to close on December 15.
